title: Callista virginea
taxonomy: Callista virginea is classified as a species of sea snail, specifically a marine gastropod mollusk in the family Calliidae. It belongs to the phylum Mollusca and the kingdom Animalia. The family Calliidae is a group of deep-sea snails, and Callista virginea is one of the species found in this family. Further classification of Callista virginea includes the kingdom Animalia, phylum Mollusca, class Gastropoda, order Trochida, and family Calliidae. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary history and relationships of the species.

intro: Callista virginea, also known as the wild violet, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the genus Callista within the family Orchidaceae. This plant is valued for its numerous health benefits, including its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Callista virginea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Callista virginea is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often employed to address issues related to the gastrointestinal system and to promote overall well-being.
